mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.25% signal zero lower bound conditions and quantitative easing expectations, reducing equity discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously when rates are near zero.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2015-07-15 indicates sustained near-zero policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth accelerates above 200,000 jobs, signaling robust labor demand and consumer spending capacity. This triggers positive equity returns within 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations rise.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline by more than 10% month-over-month, signaling weakening construction demand and forward GDP growth concerns. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations contract.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month percentage change falls below -10%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 557 thousand units as of 2015-06-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances following HOUST declines exceeding -10%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines for two consecutive months, indicating supply-side contraction and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This signals stagflation risk and produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of two-month consecutive INDPRO decline periods, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates while unemployment remains stable below 5.5%, indicating consumer confidence and spending power without wage-inflation pressure. Joint strength in demand and labor stability produces positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% AND UNRATE remains below 5.5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 355,777 million dollars and UNRATE at 9.7% as of 2015-07-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient (RSAFS × UNRATE) has t-statistic < 1.5 in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 2.5% while federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, creating negative real rates that compress equity risk premiums. This joint monetary-inflation condition produces positive 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.541 as of 2015-06-15 and FEDFUNDS at 0.11% as of 2015-07-15
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS) exhibits Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, signaling labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk. In recession-adjacent regimes, this produces negative 20-day returns; in expansions, the effect is muted.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances during recession-adjacent regimes, or Rank IC < 0.05 in recession-regime subsamples over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized while housing starts fall below 1.0 million units annualized, indicating synchronized demand weakness across consumption and investment. This produces negative 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.5% AND HOUST annualized rate falls below 1.0 million units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,155 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2015-04-15 and HOUST at 557 thousand as of 2015-06-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand slowdown or pre-recession contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient (GDPC1 × HOUST) exhibits t-statistic < 1.4 in rolling 2-year regressions.
